revision.comment: 新項目
revision.text:
{{文学}} '''車持千年'''（くらもちのちとせ、生没年不詳）は、[[飛鳥|飛鳥時代]]・[[奈良時代]]の[[歌人]]。
[[上野国]]の地方豪族である[[車持氏]]の出身。[[元正天皇]]・[[聖武天皇]]に宮廷歌人として仕えたらしく、行幸に従駕した折の歌8首が『[[万葉集]]』に掲載されている(6‐0913・0914、0931・0932、0950～0953)。
== 参考文献 == *[[中西進]]訳『万葉集 全訳注原文付』講談社、1978年。 *坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年。 *佐伯有清編『日本古代氏族事典』雄山閣出版、1994年。
{{デフォルトソート:くらもちのちとせ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:奈良時代の人物]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:没年不明]]

revision.text:
{{文学}} '''車持 千年'''（くらもち の ちとせ、生没年不詳）は、[[飛鳥時代|飛鳥]]・[[奈良時代]]の[[歌人]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は朝臣。[[主殿寮|主殿属]]・車持馬人の子で、子に門主がいたとする系図がある<ref>鈴木真年『百家系図』巻52,小池</ref>。
[[車持氏]]（車持君・車持朝臣）は[[上毛野氏]]の同族で<ref>『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京皇別</ref>、[[上野国]]の地方豪族。千年は[[元正天皇]]・[[聖武天皇]]に宮廷歌人として仕えたらしく、行幸に従駕した折の歌8首が『[[万葉集]]』に掲載されている(6‐0913・0914、0931・0932、0950～0953)。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[中西進]]訳『万葉集 全訳注原文付』講談社、1978年。 *坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年。 *佐伯有清編『日本古代氏族事典』雄山閣出版、1994年。 *宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年。
{{デフォルトソート:くらもち の ちとせ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:奈良時代の人物]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:没年不明]]

revision.text:
'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "-qq]]" to "-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]]"
revision.text:
'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

revision.text:
'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).

== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

revision.text:
'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

revision.text:
'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).

'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bump]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).

== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. '''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.

The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).

'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}

'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
'''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[court poet]] under [[Empress Genshō]] and [[Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{Normdaten}}
{{citation needed|Kuramochi Chitose}}

His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[:en:court poet|court poet]] under [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] and [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[:en:Nakanishi Susumu|Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Toshio Hōga|Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{translated page|ja|車持千年|version=94248328|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持千年]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持千年]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持千年]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0715|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|waka poet|WD=Q11122954}}|family={{ill|Kurumamochi clan|WD=Q120336431}}|given_name={{ill|Chitose|WD=Q58785425}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11637308]] Wikidata name: Kuramochi no Chitose [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]].<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[:en:court poet|court poet]] under [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] and [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[:en:Nakanishi Susumu|Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Toshio Hōga|Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持千年]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持千年]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持千年]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0715|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|waka poet|WD=Q11122954}}|family={{ill|Kurumamochi clan|WD=Q120336431}}|given_name={{ill|Chitose|WD=Q58785425}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11637308]] Wikidata name: Kuramochi no Chitose [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}}.<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[:en:court poet|court poet]] under {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō}} and {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[:en:Nakanishi Susumu|Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' {{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}}, 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Toshio Hōga|Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{translated page|ja|車持千年|version=94248328|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:車持千年]]
[[ja:車持千]]
<!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--><!--Kuramochi Chitose--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11637308}} [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:8th-century deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kurumamochi Clan]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:People of Nara-period Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持千年]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持千年]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持千年]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0715|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|waka poet|qid=Q11122954|1=Waka poet}}|family={{ill|Kurumamochi clan|qid=Q120336431|1=Kuramochi clan}}|given_name={{ill|Chitose|qid=Q58785425|1=Chitose}}}} '''Kuramochi Chitose''' (車持 千年) was a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} [[waka poet]]. His family name, ''Kuramochi'' (車持), bore the [[kabane]] title of ''Asomi'' (朝臣).
The [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持君・車持朝臣) was a branch of the [[Kamitsukeno clan]] and a local aristocratic family from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}.<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』Left Capital Imperial Lineage.</ref> Chitose served as a [[:en:court poet|court poet]] under {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}} and {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. Eight of his poems, composed during imperial processions, are included in the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|qid=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'' (6:0913–0914, 0931–0932, 0950–0953).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [[:en:Nakanishi Susumu|Nakanishi Susumu]], trans. ''Man'yōshū: Complete Annotated Translation with Original Text.'' {{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|qid=Q726081|lt=Kodansha|1=Kodansha}}, 1978. * [[Sakamoto Tarō]] and [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. ''Dictionary of Names in Japan's Ancient Clans.'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. * [[Saeki Arikiyo]], ed. ''Japanese Ancient Clans Encyclopedia.'' [[Yūzankaku Publishing]], 1994. * [[Toshio Hōga|Hōga Toshio]]. ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
